sessions.lever: consolidated GTE LZCS/LZCR block + removed pin/barrier/standalone-nops + mirrored sibling post-LZ form

reviewer.evidence: GTE block matches user-authorized sibling pattern (func_800274BC / func_8001A67C) byte-for-byte in structure; inner post-GTE locals identical to sibling; trailing block-scope on temp_v0/temp_v1_3 has 4 genuine division uses (not dead, not padding). No register pins, no scheduling barriers, no standalone cheat-asm. One transparency note: block-scope replaces the HEAD scheduling barrier on temp_v0; document in notes that the motive is grouping the 4 divisions, not RA coercion.

remaining_gap.summary: Two diffs in the /100 strength-reduce of the if-branch: (a) mfhi destination $t5 in our build vs $t8 in target (the diagnose-flagged 'mfhi/mflo synthetic intermediate' pattern), and (b) the delay-slot fill for the beqz picks addiu $v1, $zero, 0x44C first instead of target's lui $a0, hi(0x51EB851F). Both diffs live in the strength-reduce subtree where the magic constant 0x51EB851F is a GCC-synthetic temp with no source-level handle.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: If candidate were committed today, regfix.txt would still need: subst mfhi $13->$24 @ 52, subst sra $3,$13,5 -> $24 @ 53, reorder 44,45,43 @ 43-45 (post-LZ srl order — but candidate's post-LZ already mirrors sibling so may not need this), and the lui/ori/addiu delay-slot reorder. The other 4 rules (GTE block reorder, /1000 scheduling) are no longer needed because the consolidated GTE asm and the temp_v0 block-scoping handle them naturally.

next_hypotheses: Try ALLOCDBG-instrumented cc1 (tmp/gccdbg/cc1 with BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG=1) to dump the RA decision at the strength-reduce mfhi point. Identify which pseudo got which hard reg and what conflicts forced $t5 over $t8.
Try BB2_SCHED_DEBUG on the if-branch BB to see why reorg.c picks addiu instead of lui as the first fill candidate. Likely an LUID ordering issue on the synthetic magic-constant pseudo.

Try variants that introduce an unused-but-loaded constant via a sibling expression (e.g. set t2_base/t3_base AFTER the divide instead of before, so t2/t3 are alive across the divide and consume the lower temps, pushing mfhi to $t8). Risk: alias-renames a previously hoisted pointer.
Try sibling func_8002BC68 as a parallel grind — its $2<->$3 swap rule suggests a related but distinct RA cluster; cross-comparing greg dumps may reveal a shared lever.

If all in-C levers measurably exhaust, surface to user for canonical-asm cluster authorization. The GTE LZCS/LZCR pattern (and the cluster's mfhi-synthetic-intermediate symptom) is identical to the two already-authorized siblings func_8001A67C / func_800274BC; this would be a 3rd sibling under the same evidence rationale. Per HARD RULES: orchestrator-escalate, do not self-authorize.

rejected_forms.form: precompute base_diff = 0x44C - var_t0 outside the if (v1_precompute_diff.c)
rejected_forms.why_rejected: Changes the if-test from sltiu to a real compare on the precomputed diff, breaks the structure of the magic-mult subtree, and 30-distance regression — wrong shape entirely.
GCC pulls the -1 into a separate negu, adding 2 insns. +2 distance regression.

rejected_forms.form: intermediate s32 var_t0_s = (s32)var_t0 hoisted before the if (v3_intermediate_var.c)
rejected_forms.why_rejected: No effect — same as baseline. The sign cast was already inline at the use sites; hoisting it doesn't change the LUID of the magic-constant pseudo.
GCC emits an extra constant load for 0x15800 and a different mult schedule. +8 distance.
Identical RTL to baseline — GCC inlines the local at the / 100 site.
Same shape as v2; +2 from the explicit negation.
GCC normalizes the comparison; identical RTL to baseline.
